message:
test(fuzz): add github webhook payload + tailscale ACL rule shape fuzz targets

Two new libfuzzer-driven fuzz targets that exercise input boundaries
the GitHub webhook and Tailscale ACL paths previously had no coverage
on. Both follow the existing fuzz-target conventions
(`#![no_main]`, `libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target!`, structure-aware mixed
with raw-byte payloads).

1. fuzz/fuzz_targets/github_webhook_payload.rs (198 lines)

   Drives `fcp_github::types::WebhookPayload` — the typed envelope the
   GitHub connector consumes after the host has verified the
   X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC. The harness mixes raw JSON byte slices
   (capped at 32 KiB) with structure-aware valid envelopes built via
   `arbitrary::Arbitrary` derives on `WebhookRepository` and
   `WebhookSender`. Coverage focus:
   - Enum decoding under malformed event tags.
   - Optional `repository` / `sender` field handling.
   - Arbitrary `event_data: serde_json::Value` payloads.
   - Topic-derivation paths that key off the parsed event.
   - Round-trip stability of the structure-aware variant.

2. fuzz/fuzz_targets/tailscale_acl_rule_shape.rs (254 lines)

   Drives `fcp_tailscale::ZoneAclGenerator` rule generation and
   `ZoneAclRule` serde round-trip (tag.rs:266-333). The rule shape
   encodes a real security invariant: `src` tag equals every `dst`
   entry's tag prefix; `action == "accept"`; `dst` contains exactly
   the (symbol_port, control_port) pair. The fuzzer probes for
   regressions that drop or reorder dst entries — most dangerously,
   dropping the control_port restriction would expose mesh control-
   plane traffic to any zone member, defeating the defense-in-depth
   gate at tag.rs:262-264.

Wiring (fuzz/Cargo.toml):
  - Add `fcp-github = { path = "../connectors/github" }` so the
    github harness can pull `fcp_github::types`.
  - Register two new [[bin]] entries:
      `fuzz_github_webhook_payload`     -> github_webhook_payload.rs
      `fuzz_tailscale_acl_rule_shape`   -> tailscale_acl_rule_shape.rs
    Both with `test = false`, `doc = false`, `bench = false` per the
    fuzz-target convention used by the other ~120 entries in the file.

fuzz/Cargo.lock updated to record the new `fcp-github` package edge
in the fuzz workspace's resolution graph.

Workspace Cargo.lock: a single ambient one-line sync — the
`fcp-conformance` package's dep list now includes `fcp-streaming`,
matching the `fcp-streaming = { path = "../fcp-streaming" }` line
already present in `crates/fcp-conformance/Cargo.toml`. This is just
the lockfile catching up to a previously-committed Cargo.toml change;
not introduced here, only recorded.

No production code changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
